input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

  <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                  SelectionMode="Single"
                  SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
                  SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}"
                  IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                  RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

    <!-- Wrap in a RefreshView for pull-to-refresh -->
    <CollectionView.Behaviors>
      <!-- Alternative: wrap the CollectionView in a RefreshView -->
    </CollectionView.Behaviors>

  </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

The idiomatic approach is to **wrap** the `CollectionView` in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

  <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                  SelectionMode="Single"
                  SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
                  SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}">
    <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
      <DataTemplate>
        <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
          <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
          <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}"
                 TextColor="Gray" />
        </Grid>
      </DataTemplate>
    </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
  </CollectionView>

</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api, INavigationService nav)
    {
        _api = api;
        _nav = nav;
    }

    // Pull-to-refresh: reload from API, then clear the flag
    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
    }

    // Selection: navigate to detail, then clear selection
    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelectedAsync()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await _nav.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });

        SelectedItem = null;    // deselect so the user can re-tap
    }
}
```

**Key binding points:**

| XAML Property | ViewModel Property | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` | `Items` (ObservableCollection) | Drives the list |
| `SelectedItem` *(TwoWay)* | `SelectedItem` | Tracks the tap; set to `null` after navigation to allow re-selection |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | `ItemSelectedCommand` | Fires on tap → navigates |
| `RefreshView.Command` | `RefreshCommand` | Fires on pull-down → reloads data |
| `RefreshView.IsRefreshing` | `IsRefreshing` | Set `false` when done to dismiss the spinner |

**Things to watch out for:**
- `SelectedItem = null` after navigating — without this, tapping the same row twice won't fire the command again.
- `IsRefreshing = false` at the **end** of `RefreshAsync` — forgetting this leaves the spinner stuck.
- `SelectionMode="Single"` is required; the default is `None`, which disables selection entirely.
